steps.title: Identify the starting value
steps.content:
An exponential equation can be written as
$$y=a(b)^x$$
When $x=0$, the value of $y$ is $80$, so the starting value is $a=80$.

steps.title: Find the growth multiplier
steps.content: An increase of $40\%$ means each value is multiplied by $$1+0.40=1.40$$ So the model is $$y=80(1.40)^x$$ The answer is A.
